def: v.n. To overturn, tilt over, upset.
more: v. n. To overset. اڙهک پر uṛhak par, Abroach, atilt.

ety: caus. of اڙهنا uṛhnā, or اوڙهنا oṛhnā
def: v.t. To cover; to put on a covering (as a cloak, shawl, &c.); to clothe, dress; to help to dress.
more: v. a. To cause to clothe.
v. a. (dakh. caus. of اڙنا uṛnā ) To cause to fly; to steal: see اڙانا uṛānā.

def: s.m. = اٿهنگن uṭhangan, and اڙهيکن uṛhīkan, qq.v. s.m. Prop, support; anything placed under a vessel to prevent its upsetting, or under a body to keep it from rolling; a scotch-stone, wedge, &c. (also written uṭhgan).
more: s. m. Any thing placed under a vessel to prevent its oversetting; a prop, a support.

def: s.m. Covering; clothing ( = oṛhnā).
n. m. See uṭhgan .
s.m. Anything placed under a vessel to tilt it up, or to support it; prop; scotch.
s.f. Turning over, upsetting, tilting over
root: ا ڙ ه ک ن ا

def: v.t. = اوڙهنا oṛhnā, q.v. v.t. To cover with, to put on, wear; — s.m. (S. आवरण+कः), Covering, sheet, coverlet, quilt; a woman's sheet or mantle (covering the head and the upper half of the body)
more: v. a. To put on clothes. See اوڙهنا oṛhnā.
v. n. (dakh.) See اڙنا uṛnā, To fly.
subs: (-kā), To remove the covering or veil (of); to expose, defame
v.t. To cover (one) with a sheet or mantle; to marry (since, among villagers, in marrying a widow a sheet or mantle is thrown over her)

(-ke), To throw (her) mantle on the neck (of), to seek justice against (in doing which, an insulted woman, in former times, used to throw her mantle round the neck of the man who insulted her, and dragged him to the king for justice).

def: W. n. f. A mistress; a kept woman; concubine. Rāmā, biahi ke mārab, biahi ke garyāib, Uṛhrī ke guṅjrī gaṛhāib . Mag. Wom. Song. Jhūmar . O Ram! his wife he beats, his wife reviles! With jewels made for her his mistress piles!
(dakh.) i. q. غوطه مارنا g̠ẖot̤a mārnā, To plunge, to dip (?).
= A عريان ʻuryān, (v.n. fr. عري 'to be naked,' &c.), Naked, nude, bare, stripped, deprived (of), devoid (of).
adj. Out of debt, free from debt.
adj. Belonging to this side, hithermost.
v.t. To hem; to seam. (See ormā.)

ety: uṛ˚ = Prk. उड्डे(इ) or उड्ड(इ) = S. उड्डीय(ते), rt. उद्+डी
def: v.n. To fly, fly up, soar; to take flight; to move or go rapidly or quickly, to go flying along; to disappear, vanish; to be sprung; to explode (a mine); to spread, become current, be circulated (a report or rumour); to make a show or display, cut a dash; give oneself airs; to be in full bloom, to flourish; to try one's pranks or tricks (with, -se), to practise deceit or fraud (with); to be dexterously or skilfully handled, &c. (an instrument or tune)
subs: v.n. ( = uṛnā, but generally intensive, e.g.), to fly away; to run away, escape; to be wiped out, rubbed out, effaced, obliterated; to be worn out; to be lost; to be exhausted, spent; to disappear, vanish, to be carried off, stolen; to be dissipated; to escape, to evaporate (as scent, &c.); to become faded or dim; to be ravished; lose one's senses; to fly or rush (at, -par)
v.n. To walk with a stately or affected gait, to strut ( = itrānā); to grow vain or conceited (because of, or in, -meṅ)
To be in a state of bloom, to flourish.
wety: Urdu verb set اُڑْنا (uṛnā) اُڑانا (uṛānā) Inherited from Old Hindi उडना (uḍanā), from Prakrit 𑀉𑀟𑁆𑀟𑁂𑀇 (uḍḍei), from Sanskrit उड्डयते (uḍḍayate).

ety: uṛ, rt. of uṛnā + S. नागिनी
def: s.f. A flying serpent; a dragon; (Met.) A she dragon, a woman in a violent passion.

ety: v.n. fr. عرس 'to keep or cleave to,' &c.
def: s.m. Marriage, nuptials; a marriage feast; a religious ceremony celebrating the union of the soul of a deceased pīr or saint with the Supreme Spirit (the saint's disciples gather round his grave, and read the fātiḥa, and offer food, incense, lights, &c., especially on the 12th of Rabīʻuʼl-awwal); offerings to a saint; oblations; obsequies
more: n. m. عرس tied together. 1. A Mah. religious ceremony celebrating the union of the soul of the deceased Saint with his maker as a nuptial union. The disciples of a Pīr or saint assemble about his grave, and read the Fātiha and offer up food, incense, or lights, esp. on the 12th of Rabī-ul-avval . 2. ( Contemp .) Obsequies. Āj kyā dādā kā uras kīyā thā? Are you celebrating your grandfather's obsequies today? (Said to a miser who seldom entertains).
s. m. 1. Oblations, offerings to a saint. 2. A marriage feast. ¶ iﻌrs, s. m. f. A spouse.
subs: (-kā), To celebrate the union (of), to give a marriage-feast; to celebrate the obsequies (of).

def: v.t. To cause emotion; to excite the passions.
more: E. v. a. To excite the passions. Keṅchulī sabad se pȳā arasaitai ho, kaise ke bedhtaë * sarīr? Bhoj. Song. The bride, arrayed in her wedding garments, and about to set out for her husband's home, coquettishly points to the bells on her boddice and says, "When their tinkling excites my husband—"

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Flying; on the wing
subs: lit. 'To know a bird on the wing'; (prov.) to know a rook when he is seen.

def: s.m. = اتو uttū, q.v. s.m. Plaiting, puckering, crimping, gathering into ornamental plaits or folds; marking; — plaits or folds of cloth
more: s. m. Crimpling, plaits (as of clothes). See اتو uttū.
subs: v.t. To streak, mark, or wale (the body by beating), to turn (one) into a mass of wales (by severe beating; — e.g. mārkar uttū banāya uske-taʼīṅ, 'he beat him to a mummy')

v.t. To plait, pucker, crimp, &c.; to streak or wale (the body by beating = uttū banānā); to make a fool of; to stupefy, besot; to overwhelm with raillery or abuse; to vilify, disgrace, dishonour, bring into disrepute
and uttū-gar, s.m. One who plaits, &c., one who makes ornamental folds in cloth
To become silly, or stupid, or stupefied, or besotted, &c.; to be disgraced, to lose repute or character, to fall into disrepute.

def: s. m. A crimpler or plaiter (of clothes).
adj. Large, great, wide, ample
s. (dakh.) Phaseolus mungo, Lin. or black gram ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
s.m. Ascent; ascension; rising; exaltation; — zenith.
more: n. m. عرج ascended. See اونچائي 1, 2. 1. Ascent; ascension; exaltation. 2. Zenith ( simt-ul-rās ).
(from عرج To ascend, Heb. To raise) s. m. Ascent, ascension, rising.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُرُوج (urūj), from Arabic عُرُوج (ʕurūj). First attested in c. 1421 as Middle Hindi عروج ('rvj /⁠'urūj⁠/).

def: s.m. Raft; float; the moon (the half-moon being formed like a boat).
more: s. m. A raft, a float.

def: ( Ṭhagī ), n. m. A stone.
s.m. Fertile soil, yielding every kind of crop; land in general. (See ubarā).
= اربسي urbasī, q.v. s.f. Name of an Apsara or nymph of Indra's heaven; an apparition, phantom; an ornament worn on the breast.
s.f. The earth.
= A عري ʻurī (v.n. fr. عري 'to be naked,' &c.), Naked, nude, bare, stripped, deprived (of), devoid (of).

ety: ʻuryān, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Nakedness, nudity, &c.

ety: S. तस्य, gen. sing. neut., base तद्
def: adv. That bank, the further or other bank (of a river); the other side
more: adj. pro. S., अद्स, असौ, Pr. aha , Pr. and Pālī. asu , That. us bāt . An inuendo, signifying carnal intercourse. Na pāoṅ, mardue, us bāt pe bahut phailā, Merā, khudā kī qasam, in dinoṅ hai sir mailā . Wom. Nāznīn. us pār , E. oh pār . The other side (of the river). us par , adv. 1. On that; on it; on him. 2. Thereupon; accordingly; on that account. us dam, usī —, adv. The very moment; instantly. us qism kā , Of such a nature, kind, sort, fashion. us-kā, us-kī, us-ke , pro. His, her, its. us-ko, use , W. Rus. wā'he, wā'ne, bāne ; Māṛ. ऊंने, उणने, , pro. S. अस्य, , Pālī. assa . Him; her; it. us-ke nām se , Under his name. us-ke nām kā , For his name's sake; for his sake. Uske nām kā ek bāman jimā do . Hin. us ne, un ne , pro. He, she, or it.
pron. dem. sing. inflect. That, him, her, it. اس مین us meṅ, In that (time), meanwhile, mean-time.
subs: adv. On that, thereupon; accordingly; on that account
adv. On that even; for all that; still; nevertheless
usī dam, adv. That very moment, instantly, forthwith
Under his name, to his address
For his name's sake, for his sake
adv. On that, meanwhile, in the meantime (for o her comp. v.s.v. is)
adv. Free, gratis, for nothing; without cause, reason or motive, gratuitously ( = yūṅ or yoṅ).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀲𑁄 (eso), from Sanskrit एषः (eṣaḥ).

def: vice versa
her
yonder, over there
v.n. = اکسنا ukasnā, q.v. v.n. To be raised; to rise, mount, start up (in, -meṅ); to try to move, struggle to rise, to struggle; to be excited, moved, stirred, roused, &c.
s. (dakh.) Form, figure, appearance: see صورت ṣūrat.
v. n. To be agitated, to be tossed, to get into confusion.
part. adj. Confused, disordered, tossed about; agitated
subs: v.n. To be tossed, to be disordered or confused: to be agitated.

def: v.t. To separate the husk or chaff from corn (usually done by driving or blowing it away with a fan), to winnow; to sift; to pour off or away ( = usevnā, q.v.).
more: W. v. a. S. अव away, सृ to flow. To winnow; to separate the husk from the corn; drive the chaff with the fan.

def: v.t. To cause to boil; to boil. (Cf. ushannā — app. fr. rt. ush — and usannā, of which usānnā may be the caus.)
more: (trans. of اسننا) v. a. To boil.

def: see اسننا usannā.
more: v. a. 1. E. To boil; simmer ( ubālnā ). 2. W. To knead the flour ( māṅḍnā ). us'nā chāur , E. se'lā chāwal , W. n. m. The rice separated from the husk after being boiled.

def: v.n. To boil; to simmer.
s.m. = اساس usās, q.v. s.m. Breath; breathing, respiration; sigh; groan.
adj. Barren (land); — s.m. A barren spot, saline soil ( = ussar)
more: n. m. S. अवसर, Pālī, avasaro occasion. 1. Opportunity ( mauqā ); season ( rut ); chance; occasion. Ausar chūkí, ḍomnī gāve tāl be-tāl . Prov. The season passed, the songstress sings all out of tune. 2. Leisure; spare time ( fursat ). 3. Vocation; calling. 4. Difficulty; hardship; distress; misfortune. Ausar pe kām āo, jab janeṅ. ausar pānā , v. a. To get an opportunity. Khelnī hoë horī, khelye, Pherī aiso ausar nahiṅ paīhau . E. Rus. Holī Song. Come take your fill of pleasure now, 'tis Holī's carnival, No chance like this will come to you, sensuous, ecstatical. ausar dekh'nā , v. n. 1. To look out or watch for an opportunity. Mās Bhādoṅ yūṅ kahe tū baiṭhī ausar dekh, Us se chārah nā chale, jin likhī karam kī rekh . Wom. Bārā-māsā. Says August, fatal month, look out for me, The lines of fate erased you ne'er will see. 2. To expect or wait for.
s. m. Occasion, opportunity, time, leisure.
subs: usar-sāṅdā, s.m. A species of lizard found in sandy soil (see sāṅḍā).

def: s.m. Any passage for egress, porch, portico, peristyle, vestibule; verandah; shed.
more: n. m. S. अपसुतम् Pr. avasariam gone out. A thatched porch, portico, a verandah, or sun-shade. 1. Naukar ko chākar, maṅṛī ko usārā . E. Prov. A servant to a servant, a shed to a hut! (The one is as absurd as the other). 2. In galyan meṅ saram lagat hai, Le chal, murhā, usāre meṅ, Merī baīyāṅ na chhūo galyāre meṅ . Rus. Song. Here in the lane I feel ashamed, Take me, idiot, to you shed, Now in the lane don't touch my arm.
s. m. A porch, portico, peristyle, vestibule.

ety: for A. عصارة, v.n. fr. عصر 'to press'
def: s.m. Expressed juice; extract; choice part; — dregs (after the juice is expressed), refuse

ety: S. अप, अव or उद्+सारणं, rt. स्ट
def: v.t. To remove, put out of place, displace; to finish, complete; to finish expeditiously.
more: E. v. a. To complete; finish; do properly; to do or finish quickly.
v. a. To remove, to put out of place.

ety: S. अप+सरणं, rt. सृः cf. the tr. form usārnā
def: v.n. To retreat, recede; to shrink from; to be removed or displaced; to be finished or completed with expedition.
more: v. n. To retreat, recede, shrink from.

def: s.m. Breath; breathing, respiration; sigh; groan.
more: n. m. S. उत up, श्वस् to breathe. Breath; breathing; respiration ( sāṅs ).
s. (dakh.) A groan (heavy: Harris ). اساسان بهرنا usāsān bharnā (dakh.) To sigh.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اساس (asās), from Arabic أَسَاس (ʔasās).

def: s. (dakh). See اساس usās.
v.n. To breathe, respire, to live, have existence.
more: v. n. To breathe; respire; live; have existence.

def: s.m. A week.
pron. object. case of وه wuh, q. v.
s.f. Remembering with regret; grieving, pining or fretting (for), yearning (after); grief, vexation, anxiety, solicitude ( = auser)
subs: (-kī), To think (of) with regret; to grieve, pine or fret (for); to be anxious (about); to be grieved, vexed or displeased.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسِیر (asīr), from Arabic أَسِير (ʔasīr).

def: v. n. S. उप् + स्मृ to remember. 1. To bear or keep in mind; to retain in the memory. 2. To yearn for; languish; pine; feel the want of. Bachchā mā kī user kartā hai . 3. To be displeased, offended, angry. Mujhe panṛā ko jānā, balam kareṅge user . Wom. Song. I must go to the well (for water), or my husband will be angry.

v.t. = اسانا usānā, q.v.: cf. also ulaćnā. v.t. To separate the husk or chaff from corn (usually done by driving or blowing it away with a fan), to winnow; to sift; to pour off or away ( = usevnā, q.v.).
more: v. a. To throw out the water from boiled rice.

def: s.m. Safflower, bastard saffron, Carthamus tinctorius, or Cnicus.

ety: said to be fr. عصي وفرّ 'to disobey and fly'

interj. (dakh.) See اف uf.
s.f. Morning light, dawn, daybreak, morning; the wife of Aniruddha, son of Kām-deva
more: s. f. 1. The wife of Aniruddha, son of Kāmadeva. 2. Early morn, daybreak.

s.m. Black pepper.

adj. Barren, &c. = ūsar, q.v. adj. Barren (land); — s.m. A barren spot, saline soil ( = ussar)

def: s.f. = اشا ushā, q.v. s.f. Morning light, dawn, daybreak, morning; the wife of Aniruddha, son of Kām-deva

def: s.m. Sarsaparilla.
s.m. Lit. 'That with which one is diverted'; tale, story, fable; fiction, fabrication, lie.
more: Pop. n. m. Fiction; fable; lie; fib; white lie; story; fabrication. Phr. The thing that is not. Roz ek nayā ushgulā chhoṛ-ke dāl jūtī baṭwātī hai . Wom.

def: adj. Hot
subs: s.m. Lit. 'Heat-born'; any animal generated by heat (as gnats, flies, insects in a dunghill, &c.)

def: adj. Hot
subs: s.m. Lit. 'Heat-born'; any animal generated by heat (as gnats, flies, insects in a dunghill, &c.)

s.m. Heat, warmth; the hot season; — adj. Hot, warm; pungent, acrid.
s.f. Heat, warmth ( = ushṇa).
s.m. Turban; diadem.
s.m. A tenth part, a tithe
subs: The tithes prescribed by divine law
The tenth of a tenth, a hundredth part; (fig.) very little.

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻāshiq, q.v.), Lovers.
s.m. Camel ( = H. ūṅṭh; P. ushtur and shutur).
s.m. Fierceness, violence, taking anything by force, injustice; celerity; — adj. Firm.
more: s. m. Fierceness, violence, taking any thing by force, injustice. adj. Firm.

def: s.m. A camel ( = شتر shutur, the com. form).
v.t. To boil, to cook by boiling or simmering; — v.n. To be boiled, to be dressed or cooked over a slow fire ( = usijnā).
more: (caus. of اسجنا usijnā ) v. a. To boil.

ety: S. उद्+सिचनं or perhaps from rt. श्री
def: v.n. To bubble up, boil, boil up; to simmer, to be boiled or cooked over a slow fire.
v.t. To boil, to cook by boiling or simmering; — v.n. To be boiled, to be dressed or cooked over a slow fire ( = usijnā).
more: W. Mah. v. n. S. उपश्री lit , to under-cook, from उप् + श्री to cook. To be dressed over a slow fire (after the mess is three parts done); to simmer.
(caus. of اسجنا usijnā ) v. a. To boil.

more: n. f. S. उशीर A grass, the scented roots of which are used for making ṭaṭṭies; Andropogon muricatum . Wat.
s. f. A fragrant root of grass, used in making ṭaṭṭīs. (Andropogon muricatum. Pers. خس ḵẖas ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَسِیر (asīr), from Arabic أَسِير (ʔasīr).

def: s.f. = اسير usīr, q.v. s.f. A grass, the fragrant roots of which are used for making ṭaṭṭis, Andropogon muricatum (syn. ḵẖas-ḵẖas).
s.m. Pillow, bolster; cushion; head of a bed ( = sirānā).
more: s. m. Pillow, cushion, head of a bed or resting place.

def: v.t. = اکسانا uksānā, q.v. v.t. To raise, lift; to trim (a lamp, by raising the wick); to kindle (a fire), fan (a flame), blow (fire), stir up (embers); to rouse, stir up, move, excite, stimulate, urge; to encourage, incite, instigate.
more: v. a. To light a fire or candle; to excite, to instigate.

def: s.m. Order, arrangement; manner, mode, method; course, way, manner of acting or conduct; form, figure, shape (cf. ḵẖush-uslūb)
more: (or vulg. aslūb ) s. m. Manner, mode, method: order, arrangement: form. اسلوبدار uslūb-dār, adj. Well-arranged, well-proportioned, elegant.
subs: adj. Methodical; well-arranged; well-proportioned, symmetrical; elegant.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أُسْلُوب (ʔuslūb).

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عُثْمَان (usmān), from Arabic عُثْمَان (ʕuṯmān).

def: Ottoman
wety: From عُثْمان ('usmān, “Usman”) + ـی (-ī)

more: (caus. of اسننا usannā ) v. a. To cause to boil.

institutional, systematic

def: s.f. Difficulty, hardness; intricacy; straitness; distress, poverty.
adj. = اوسر ūsar, q.v. adj. Barren (land); — s.m. A barren spot, saline soil ( = ussar)
more: Mag. adj. S. ऊषर, ऊष salt ground. Impregnated with salt, or saltpetre; barren (land). Ussar khet meṅ kesar? E. Prov. You can't grow saffron in a barren soil.
subs: usar-sāṅdā, s.m. A species of lizard found in sandy soil (see sāṅḍā).

def: s.m. A barber (respectfully used for nāʼī).
more: Mah. n. m. A respectful term for a barber ( nāī ). Ustā, hajām, nāī, maiṅ, merā bhāī, ghoṛī, ghoṛī kā bachchā, aur mujhe to āp jānte hī haiṅ. Latīfa . Witticism. Ustā, hajām, nāī , myself, my brother, my mare, her foal, and me of course you know. ☞ This saying is founded on a story told of a barber who represented himself under different names, and by his wit obtained eight shares instead of one.
(perhaps for استاد) s. m. A barber.

ety: old P. vśtāt; Pehl. ǒśtāt; Zend aiwisti, rt. śtā = S. sthā+abhi
def: s.m. A master; craftsmaster, skilful man, adept, expert; preceptor, tutor, teacher; professor; clever rogue, sharper; tutor of a dancing girl, musician; — adj. Expert, proficient, skilful, clever
more: n. m. 1. A teacher; instructor; tutor; master, preceptor; professor; adept; top-sawyer ( slang ); master of one's trade. 2. One skilled in any art, or science; a proficient; a veteran. Jāë ustād khālī hai . Prov. The master's chair is always vacant, i. e . no one is master. 3. A sharper; a clever rogue; tactician; old stager; a sharp blade; a consummate knave. 4. A musician; the tutor of a dancing girl. Ustāj jī, tablā bāṅdho . Wom. ustād , adj. Versed in; proficient; accomplished. ustād ho'nā , v. n. To master; make oneself master of; to be a proficient; to be perfect, or versed in.
s. m. A master (in any art), an adept, teacher, preceptor, artist.
subs: To become or make onself master (of), to master, be or becom proficient (in).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اُسْتَاد (ustād). Doublet of اُسْتاذ (ustāz).

def: s. m. See استاد ustād.

more: v. n. 1. To be stopped, prevented, checked, restrained; to stick; stop. Gāṛī aṭkī chahal pahal meṅ, gorī aṭkī Gujrāt . E. Song. The cart stuck in the mire, the belle at Gujrat. 2. To stay; delay; linger; loiter. Gaë Kaṭak, rahe aṭak . Prov. Went to Kaṭak, there he stuck. 3. To hesitate; doubt; hold back. Aṭkegā, so bhaṭkegā . Prov. To doubt is to be lost. 4. To be caught (by a nail), stuck. 5. To stick in the throat. Bachche binā halaq meṅ ṭukṛā aṭaktā hai . Without my child by me, this morsel sticks in my throat. 6. To spell one's words; to be unable to read fluently. Abhī paṛhne meṅ aṭakte ho . 7. To be unrealized (money). Qist kā rupayā aṭak gayā . 8. To be bound (to an employer); to obtain employment. 9. To be locked up in (as money). Aṭkā banyā saudā de? Prov. The banyā sold, wont sell. [A burnt child dreads the fire]. Or, (without the interrogatory), Owe the Baniā a little, and he'…

adj. High up the leg, drawn up, too short (as trousers, &c.); shrivelled, shrunken; badly made or shaped.

adj. uṭhā tāṅg raised above the leg. S. टङ्ग leg. 1. Shrivelled; shrunken; gone up; too short. Chashm-i-bad dūr, Shekh jī sāhab, Kyā izār āp kī uṭaṅgī hai ! Inshā. May Heaven avert for aye the evil eye from these, Shekh jī ! These trousers, for your legs too short, are beautiful to see! 2. Badly cut or shaped.
s. m. Haste, rashness, precipitation.
adj. The lowest, least, most inferior (of, — se, e.g. Singāroṅ-meṅ jo sab-se hai wǒh utār)
more: n. m. S. उत + तृ to cross, pass. 1. Descent; slope; declivity; dip; fall; depression; down hill; declension; declination. 2. Subsidence; ebb-tide; low water. 3. Decrease; diminution; abatement; falling off; wane; decline. 4. A fall or reduction in price. Remission of revenue. 5. Scale of decrease in the rate of revenue levied from different qualities of soil. 6. A quarrelsome person. Baiṭhī laṛe wo baiṭhe se; chaltī sawār se. Mūṅ chaṛh-ke bolūṅ nauj maiṅ aisī utār se . Wom. Rāhat. Sitting she fights with those who sit, stirring with those who stir. Never a word would I exchange with such a wrangling jade. 7. A bad or shameless woman; a bad character. Yeh aurat baṛī utār hai . 8. A copy; a duplicate (A. musannā ). 9. A ford or ferry; the fare paid at a ferry.
s. m. Descent, declination. اتار دینا utār denā, To bring down; (met.) to disgrace, to dishonour.
subs: s.m. Descent and ascent, undulation (of land), ups and downs (of a road, or of fortune); decrease and increase, rise and fall, fluctuation (of prices, &c.); remission and exacerbation (of fever, anger, intoxication, &c.); ebb and flow (of a tide); higher and lower notes (of music); ascending and descending (scale); vicissitudes (of fortune); variation, alternation of hope and fear, or of loss and gain, or of good and evil, &c.; — shift, subterfuge, artifice, trick, fraud, deception
v.t. To make artful, wily, &c. suggestions; to cozen, deceive
v.t. To practise shifts, subterfuges, &c.; to plan, scheme; to cozen, cheat
s.m. Alleviation, relief, ease, comfort; remedy; discharge, liquidation (of a debt); fulfilment (of a vow, &c.).

def: s. m. See اتر ut-tar.

n. m. 1. Ascent and descent; ups and downs (of a road, etc.) ( ūṅch nīch ). 2. Ascending and descending (scale); the upper and lower notes (of music). 3. A rise and fall; increase and decrease (of price); rising and sinking. Bhāo kā utār chaṛhāo . 4. Increase and decrease (of intoxication, swoon, illness, anger, etc.). 5. Ebb and flow; ebb tide and flood tide. Daryā kā utār chaṛhāo . 6. Hope and fear; loss and gain; good and evil. 7. Change; alteration; revolution. 8. The instability of human affairs; the wheel of fortune; ups and downs of life; the vicissitudes of time. Dunyā kā utār chaṛhāo . 9. Scheme; artifice; plot; machination. Hazār aql se ham karte haiṅ utār chaṛhāo, Nazar jo unki chaṛhe haiṅ, woh kab utarte haiṅ? Mukhaīyar. 10. Trick; cheat; swindle; chouse ( dam ). Āp unke utār chaṛhāo meṅ ā-kar kuchh pūj na baiṭhyegā . Take care you are not beguiled by his oily gammon. utār' chaṛhāo de'nā,——batā'nā , a. v. To deceive; cheat; impose, practise, or play upon. Phr. To play a trick; to practise on one's credulity.
The intensive form of utārnā , q. v.

v. n. from H. utarnā . To be paid off, or discharged (a debt); to be clear, square, even with; to settle; balance, or square accounts with; to be quits; to pay off old scores; to give as good you get.
n. m. 1. Alleviation; relief; ease. 2. Liquidation; discharge (of a debt); quittance.

s.m. Descent (to a river); crossing, passage (of a river or defile); ford, ferry, wharf; ferriage; dismounting (of cavalry to fight on foot); deduction, remission; — answer, reply ( = uttar); copy, transcript, duplicate; fac-simile, counterpart; halting-place, stage (of a journey), station (for travellers); — offering (to evil spirits, esp. masān, placed at the junction of cross-roads); delivering or freeing from demoniacal possession (by moving cooked rice, flour, &c. over the length of the body of the possessed); the articles used in the ceremony of dispossessing of an evil spirit; removing (a sickness) by drugs or charmed substances, (also) the substances employed for the purpose; ransom; self-devotion; land presented to a temple; grant of land (by Government) at a quit-rent in return for services rendered; house-rent; — cast-off clothes, second-hand or worn things
more: n. m. 1. Deduction; remission. 2. The cooked rice, flour, etc. moved an odd number of times from the head to the foot of a sick person under the belief that the evil spirit is thus removed from the sick person into the rice, etc. 3. An offering made to Masān , an evil spirit believed to haunt cross-roads. 4. Station; halting place for travellers; temporary residence ( paṛāo ). Dunyā utāre kī jagah hai . 5. A ghat ; ferry; wharf; quay. 6. E. A copy; duplicate ( naql ). 7. E. An answer; reply; response ( uttar ). 8. House rent; ferry toll. 9. Land given to a temple. 10. Land given by Government to an individual as a reward for services at a quit-rent. utā'rā utār'nā , v. a. To transfer the evil spirit from the sick patient to the utārā , q. v.; to exorcise. utāre kā jhop'ṛā , n. m. 1. A common hut or shed for the use of travellers. 2. ( Met .) Applied to the body and to the world. Ye tan j…
(from اتارنا utārnā, q. v.) s. m. A descent, crossing or passing (over a river, &c): an answer: cast off clothes: disgrace, degradation: ransom, self-devotion.
subs: v.t. To dispossess of an evil spirit by means of the utārā
(-par), To descend (to); to effect the passage (of a river, &c.); to dismount (as cavalry, to fight on foot)
s.m. A hut or shed for the accommodation of travellers; (Met.) the world; the body
s.m. Warehoused goods; second-hand goods, cheap things.

v.s.v. اتار utār.

v.t. To cause to pass over, to convey over or across, transport over, ferry across, land; to cause to descend or alight; help to dismount; to bring down, take down, take off, remove, put away (from, -se); to tear off, strip off, cut or break off (a portion of anything); to lower, depress, abase, render vile, unclean or impure; to cast down, dethrone; to cast out, expel (an evil spirit); to draw down and confine (an evil spirit, in, -meṅ); to remove, or free from (fever, intoxication, &c.); to quit oneself of (a debt, obligation, oath, vow, &c.); to make a recompense or return; to unload, discharge (cargo, &c.); to disembark; to lodge, put up (a traveller); to throw aside, cast off (shame, &c.); to draw off (liquor); to make a channel (for water to flow down or off); to let down, lay (in a grave); to loosen the strings of, unstring (a guitar, &c.); to uncock (a gun); to vent (rage or spleen, on, -par); to extract (colour); to dispense, distribute, allot (sustenance); to send down (a prophet, or a gift, from heaven)
more: v. a. lit . To bring or take down. 1. To lower; depress. Nīche utārnā . 2. To haul; bring down ( kheṅchnā ). Kankavvā utār lo . 3. To cause to alight, or descend; help to dismount. Sawārī utārnā . 4. To cause to pass over; convey across; ferry over; transport; carry. 1. Utāre Nāth pār morī naīyā . Wom. Song. 2. Āṭ kāṭ kī naīyā banāī, Maulā utāre beṛā pār . Song. Barsātī . Frail is the boat made up of chips, Heaven transport it safe across. 5. To lodge; house; put up. Musāfir utārnā . 6. To drive home (baiṭhānā) ; worm in; thrust in; put in ( ghuseṛnā ). Itnī uṅglī utār dī . (Slang). 7. To let out; make a channel (for the escape of water). Pānī, yā parnālā utārnā . 8. To dethrone; cast down. Gaddī se utārnā . 9. To take down (a sick man) from the bed to the floor in his last moments. (A Hindū custom). Bhūṅ meṅ utār lȳā, ab ās nahīṅ . Hin. 10. To make a thing go down with one. Bāt gale ut…

(caus. of اترنا) v. a. 1. To cause to alight or descend, to bring down; to degrade. 2. To cause to pass over, to convey over. 3. To take off, to strip off, to tear off, cut off or break off (a portion of any thing), to unload, disembark, dismount, discharge. 4. To make a return or recompense. اتاري هوئي utāre hūʼe, Engaged with a desperate resolution in any enterprise (metaphor taken from cavalry, who dismount to fight on foot with a determination to conquer or die). کپڙي اتارنا kapṛe utārnā, To take off the clothes, to undress.
subs: v.t. To swallow or gulp down
v.t. To deprive of honour, disgrace, degrade, dethrone; to lower in the regard (of, -se); to put to shame, cause to blush; to put out (of caste); to put out, dislocate (a limb or bone
however, utār denā is identical with, or slightly intensifies, the verb utārnā)
v.t. To take off; take down, pull down; to take out, remove, rub out (colour, &c.; — cf. utārnā, of which utār ḍālnā is an intensive form)
v.t. (Intensive of utārnā, q.v. for other senses), to take down, enter, insert, record (in, -meṅ)

and utāre ho, part. adj. & adv. Dismounted; alighting, on foot; engaged with desperate resolution in any enterprise; determined, desperate (a metaphor taken from cavalry dismounting to fight on foot with a determination to conquer or die).
wety: Urdu verb set اُتَرْنا (utarnā) اُتارْنا (utārnā) اُتَرْوانا (utarvānā) Causative form of اُتَرْنا (utarnā) .

ety: utar˚ = Prk. उत्तर(इ) = S. उत्तर(ति), rt. उद्+तॄ
def: v.n. To pass over or across, go across; to come down, descend, alight, land, disembark, dismount; to make one's appearance (in, -meṅ, a place, or on a scene); to come into the world, have being, be born; to be incarnate; to be revealed; to be taken down, lowered, reduced, disgraced, degraded, deposed, dethroned; to decrease, abate, subside, fall off, wane, fade, decay, decline; to become old and worn, become wan or pale or thin; to fall (in price, value, estimation or dignity); to go down or off, sink, pass off (as fever, intoxication, &c.); to be delivered or freed (from debt, an obligation, an oath, a vow, possession by an evil spirit, &c.); to halt, encamp (in or on, -meṅ); to take (to, -par); to come out, turn out to be, to prove; to vary, change: (for other meanings see utar-jāna below). — utar-paṛnā (-meṅ, -par), v.n. To alight or settle (at or upon), to descend, come down; to fall (on or upon); to put up (in or at), to halt (at a place, on a journey). — utartā ćāṅd, s.m. The waning moon; the wane of the moon. — utar-jānā ( = utarnā in most of its meanings), v.n. To have got beyond (-se) middle age, to be growing old; to become faded, dim (as colour); to become pallid or emaciated or drawn (the face); to become insipid or flat; to be displaced or dislocated (a bone); to be taken down, pulled down, demolished; to pass away, die.
more: v. n. 1. To descend; come or go down; alight; get down; drop; drop down. Āsmān se utrā, khajūr meṅ aṭkā . Prov. Come down from the skies, stuck in the palm tree. 2. To be lowered, or reduced; to be taken down. Tawā utar gayā . 1. The cake pan has been taken down, i. e. the cakes have been baked. 2. The cake pan is not hot enough. 3. To be taken, gulped down, or swallowed ( nigalnā ). 1. Halaq se ṭukṛā nahīṅ utartā . The food wont go down (my) throat. 2. Utrā ghāṭī, huā māṭī . Prov. Down the throat, turned to clay. 4. To be thrust in, pushed in, driven home. Peṅch ghar meṅ utar gayā . The screw has been driven home. 5. To pass over; go across; cross. Pār utrā . 6. To come into the world; to be born; to become incarnate; to be revealed. 1. Jitnī le-kar utrā thā, utnā hī jȳā . Prov. saying. He lived as many days as he was born to. 2. Nabī utre. Autār utre. Kitāb utrī . 7. To come away; iss…

v. n. To descend, to alight, to halt, bait, disembark, land, dismount, to subside, to decrease, to pass over, to cross, to go off, to fall off, to decay, to fade, to flag, to be freed from debt, to become insipid, to flatten, to fall in value or in dignity.
v. n. or a. (dakh.) To descend, alight, disembark, to land, &c.

Get off the horse and come with me.

def: s.f. Haste, hurry, quickness, speed, despatch; precipitancy; eagerness, impatience; restlessness, fidgetiness
more: W. Hin. n. S. त्वर् to hurry. 1. Restlessness; fidget; hurry; haste; flurry. Dādā to he ke tāulī paṛī saë? Rus. Father, why do you hurry so? 2. Precipitancy; precipitation; impatience. 3. Quickness; speed; despatch. Chkorā, tāolī āȳo, dūd kā belā āyo . Rus. Boy, come back soon, it is time for milking. utāval kar nā , E. Rus. tā'val k . W. Rus. jaldī k . Pop. v. n. To make haste; hurry; hurry-skurry.
subs: v.n. To make haste, to hurry, &c.; to be hasty, rash, precipitate, &c.

def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Swift, quick, speedy; precipitate, hasty, rash; uneasy, fidgety, restless, impatient
more: W. n. m. A precipitate, restless, impatient person; one who hurries and flurries. Utāolā, so bāolā; dhīrā, so gambhīrā . E. Prov. Haste is madness; patience, wisdom.
adj. m. Swift, quick, speedy, precipitate, rash.

def: s.f. = اتاول utāwal, q.v. s.f. Haste, hurry, quickness, speed, despatch; precipitancy; eagerness, impatience; restlessness, fidgetiness
more: adj. f. Swift, &c.; s. f. Haste, speed, rashness, impatience.
subs: v.n. To make haste, to hurry, &c.; to be hasty, rash, precipitate, &c.

adv. As many as that, so many.

def: v. n. 1. To get up; arise; wake up; sit up; shake off sleep or drowsiness; to spring out of bed; to start to one's feet. Woh khuṛkā sunte-hī, uṭh baiṭhā . 2. To revive; recover; acquire new life and strength. Dawā pīte-hī woh uṭh baiṭhā . 3. To be brought to one's senses; to be made straight. Machlā paṛā thā, mār khāte-hī uṭh baiṭhā . He feigned and lay down, but he soon got up when he felt the lash.
Gaṛh. n. m. Anything placed under a vessel to prevent its over-setting; a prop; support. Ghaile kā uṭhgan, geṅṛūrī re, geṅṛūrī . Mag. Song.

v. n. The emphatic form of uṭhnā , q. v. 1. To come to an end; to be settled, decided. Inke āne se sab jhagṛā uṭh gayā . 2. To remove; go away; depart. 3. To cease; to become extinct; to die. 4. ( Thagi ) To be killed or hanged. uṭh khaṛ'ā ho'nā , v. n. To stand up; to rise suddenly. Baiṭhe baiṭhe kyoṅ uṭh khaṛe hue ?
s. f. Act of frequently rising up and sitting down; inconstancy.

s.f. = اٿهہ بيٿهہ uṭh-baiṭh. q.v. s.f. Rising up and sitting down, not sitting still; restlessness, fidgetiness, agitation; tossing and tumbling; perturbation, discomposure; a kind of exercise among athletes; standing and kneeling alternately at prayer; sitting and rising alternately holding both ears the while, — a mode of punishment in Indian schools
subs: v.n. To be constantly getting up and sitting down from uneasiness of mind; to suffer from restlessness; to have the fidgets.

def: To seduce, mislead, deceive (?).
v.s.v. اٿهانا uṭhānā.
s.m. Lit. 'Who picks up what is fallen or lying; pilferer, purloiner, petty thief.
more: s. m. A pilferer, a purloiner, a petty thief.

def: n. m. H. gir fall, uṭhā take up. One who takes up what has fallen. 1. A pilferer; shop-lifter; a petty thief. Phr. The light fingered gentry; thimble rigger. 2. Facetiously , a beggar.
Uthal (a city in Lasbela district, Balochistan, Pakistan)
wety: Probably from Arabic أثَالْ.

def: part. adj. (from uthalnā), Upset, &c. The commoner form is uthal-puthal, or utthal-putthal, upset, capsized, overturned; topsy-turvy, in utter confusion, in complete disorder
more: adj. (dakh.) Shallow, low ( Harris ): see اتهلا uthlā.
subs: To be upset or overturned; to be topsy-turvy; to be disordered (the stomach).

def: s.m. One who is without any fixed abode; a person not to be depended upon.
E. n. S. उत् + स्था stand. One who has no fixed abode; an unsteady unreliable person. Woh to uṭhallū hai, uskā kyā ṭhīk .
v.t. = uthlānā, q.v.
more: v. n. To turn over, to overset. اتهلنا پتهلنا uthalnā puthalnā, the same.
subs: v.t. emphat. form of uthlānā, q.v.

def: v.s.v. اتهل uthal (2).
more: adj. Overturned; upset. —adv. Upside down; topsy-turvy; in complete disorder; in utter confusion. uth'al puth'al ho'nā , v. n. 1. To be overturned, upset, topsy-turvy. 2. To be sick at stomach; to feel nausea. Nirne * mūṅ mūlī mat khā, jī uthal puthal hogā .

def: s.m. Area, court; — s.f. Act of appearing, rising, rise, ascension, ascent; height, elevation, eminence, altitude; top, summit; high range or flight; length, stature, tallness; make, build; springing up, growth; rising or swelling (of the breasts in girls); sexual desire, salacity (of the female); conceit or pride arising from sudden elevation (in rank, &c.); expense, outlay, expenditure
more: s. m. 1. An area or court. 2. Rising, ascension, appearing.
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Of high range or flight, soaring to a great height (as a bird).

def: v.t. To lift, take up, raise, raise up, elevate, hoist; to take up (anything held sacred) for the purpose of swearing by it, to swear by; to take up (one's effects, &c.) preparatory to moving or marching, (hence) to close work, break up; to put away, pack up; to start, set off, &c.; to rear, rear up, bring up, train, educate; to hatch, breed; to produce, invent, fabricate; to erect, build up, construct; to rouse (from sleep), to awaken; to support, bear, carry; to take upon oneself, bear the burden or responsibility of, undertake; to undergo, experience, suffer, endure; to incur (risk, expense, liability, &c.); to contract (debts); to obtain goods on credit (from, -se); to derive, acquire, get, gain, obtain (as the result of labour); to enjoy; to pick up; to gather in (as a harvest); to withdraw, remove (from, -se); to bear or carry forth or away, make off or away with, to steal; to cause to disappear or vanish, to consume, dispose of, dissipate, squander; to take away or remove (from the world), to translate; to clear or clean up; to finish, prepare; to efface, erase, expunge; to abolish, do away with; to eject, expel; to raise up (so as to be seen), to exhibit; to throw up, cause to rise or fly (as a paper-kite or a pigeon); to set apart, put aside (for any purpose)
more: (caus. of اٿهنا) v. a. To lift, take or raise up, to elevate, to hoist, to take or bear away, to produce, invent, cause, gain, get, enjoy, to abolish, excite, rouse, support, bear, carry, to remove, to receive, to suffer, to exhibit, to contract debts or purchase on credit, to expend. اٿها دینا uṭhā denā, To remove away, abolish, abrogate, exterminate.
subs: or baiṭhānā, v.t. To lead astray, cause to err; to excite, stir up, instigate
(intens. of uṭhānā), v.t. To lift up, raise up; to cause to vanish; to efface; to consume, dissipate, squander; to remove; to eject; to abolish; to make over, give, bestow, &c.
v.t. To take upon oneself, bear the burden or responsibility of; to put by
v.t. To take up, raise, lift up; to pick up; to take off (from, -se), to remove (from); to undertake the management or care of (see uṭhānā, of which uṭhā lenā is an intensive)
v.t. To run off or away with, to carry off; to abduct, kidnap.
wety: Urdu verb set اُٹھْنا (uṭhnā) اُٹھانا (uṭhānā) اُٹْھوانا (uṭhvānā) Transitive form of اُٹھ (uṭh) .

def: v. a. S. उत्था to stand up; उत् up, ष्ठा to stand. 1. To lift; take or raise up; give a lift; raise; exalt; rear; uprear; uplift; elevate; hoist. Bojh mere sir par uṭhā de . 2. To set up; erect; stick up. Jhanḍā yā bāoṭā uṭhānā . 3. To cause to stand up, or rise. Ek biṭhātā hai, ek uṭhātā hai . 4. To build; construct; pile; heap; erect. Kaṅkaṛ chun chun mahal uṭhāyā. Log kaheṅ ghar merā re; Nā ghar merā, nā ghar terā, Chiṛyā rain baserā re . Kabīr. With stones picked up a house is raised. And folks tell you, "this house is mine." The house is neither mine nor thine: A roosting place for birds by night." 5. To pick up. Murgī dānā uṭhā letī hai . 6. To drag; dredge; fish up. 7. To take up (to heaven). Bhagwān ne chhore ko uṭhā lȳā . Hin. Wom. 8. To fly (a kite); uncage; let fly (a bird) ( uṛānā ). Kabūtar uṭhāo . 9. To lift up; unveil; uncover; expose; lay open. Pardah uṭhā do . 10. (A double entendre ). Ek uṭhāūṅ, yā donoṅ? 11. To raise (one's hand, stick, etc. as if to strike). Lāṭhī uṭhānā . 12. To exert one's self; take trouble; labour; work hard. Mehnat uṭhānā . 13. To take on oneself; assume (responsibility). A kelī jān ne sārā ghar uṭhā rakkhā hai . 14. To begin; commence; lead the way; set on foot. Uṭhāo—maiṅ bhī gāūṅgī . Wom. 15. To pack up (for a march or journey). Nasīm, jāgo, kamar ko bāṅdho, uṭhāo bistar, ki rāt kam hai . Nasīm. 16. To have the care of; manage; superintend; administer. Ghar uṭhā lenā . 17. To turn out; prepare; finish. Chār ghaṛī meṅ ek kurtā uṭhāyā . 18. To get; gain; receive; enjoy. Mazā uṭhānā . Pīsā pīsā, chapnī bhar uṭhāyā . Prov. She ground and ground, a handful found. 19. To suffer; support; endure; bear with; brook. Dukh uṭhānā . 20. To incur expense; lay out money. Kharch uṭhānā . 21. To spend; disburse; incur (expense). Shādī (yā muqadme) meṅ atgat uṭhāyā . 22. To consume; waste; lavish. Sab rupayā uṭhā dȳā . 23. To incur (debt). Qarz uṭhānā . 24. To buy or take on credit; have an account with. Kis banye se uṭhāte ho? 25. To advance (money); supply (funds). Chār lāo uṭhā rakkhī haiṅ . 26. To give away; bestow; grant; make over. Ham ko bhī rupayā uṭhā do . 27. To remove; erase; rub out. Harf uṭhānā . 28. To resign; give up; leave; forbear; relinquish. Aisī bāt se hāth uṭhāo . 29. To close; give up (a shop). Dukān uṭhā dī . 30. To cast away; remove; clear away. 31. To abrogate; exterminate; do away with; abolish; cancel; nullify. Dastūr uṭhā dȳā . 32. To break up; put on the shelf; put aside. Bas uṭhāo, pāṅch baj gaë . 33. To lay by; put up; set apart. Mātā kā uṭhāonā uṭhāo, turat arām hogā . 34. To shift; transfer; convey; carry; bear. Pālkī uṭhānā . 35. To rob; steal; pilfer; make off with. 36. To remove; dislodge; transplant; stow away; clear. 37. To eject; expel; put out; empty; oust; turn out; evict. Asāmī uṭhā dī . 38. To depopulate; to remove (the inhabitants). 39. To wake from sleep. Sote ko mat uṭhā . 40. To invent; fabricate. Ushgulā uṭhānā . 41. To stir up; foment; kindle. 42. To rouse; excite; set on; provoke. Baiṭhe biṭhāë sotī rāṛ uṭhāī . Hin. 43. To quicken, or mend one's pace. Pāoṅ uṭhā-ke chalo . 44. To carry in a procession; exhibit. Baṛī dhūm dhām se tāzyā uṭhāyā . 45. To hatch; bring forth; breed. Murgī ne sab bachche uṭhāë . E. 46. To bring up; teach; train. Bachche ko jaisā uṭhāoge, waisā uṭṭhegā . 47. To eat up; finish; lick up. Jhūṭ mat chhoṛo, sab uṭhā lo . 48. To absorb; imbibe; suck up. Deṛ-pā peṭ kā sādā savāyā ghī uṭhātā hai . ( Halwāī's idiom.) 49. To gather (the harvest or crop). Khet uṭhā-ke ghar bhar lȳā . 50. To winnow; thresh. Pairī uṭhānā . 51. To swear by; take an oath on. Uṭhā jā gangā jalī, ham ne bhar pāyā. uṭhā le jānā , v. a. 1. To run away with; carry off. 2. To abduct; kidnap. uṭhā'wal baisā'wal , Tir. n. The rite of raising and dropping the bridegroom by the ear three times successively. (This office is performed by the bride's brother.)

ety: v.n. of उठंगना q.v.+ल = S. aff. आलू
def: adj. Dull, heavy, stupid, thick-skulled, deficient in sense or understanding, senseless, wrong-headed, silly, absurd
more: adj. (dakh. i. q. بي طور) Unmannerly, rude, indiscreet.
subs: s.m. Dull, stupid fellow, dolt, witling, dotard, a person devoid of all judgment, sense or discretion; a poor creature; a mere animal, a beast
uṭhangal-mulk. s.m. An ill-governed state or kingdom
a land of anarchy and misrule.

def: E. adj. 1. Dull; heavy; silly; absurd; stupid; wrong-headed; thick-skulled; senseless; deficient in understanding. uṭhaṅ'gal ādmī , E. n. m. A dull, stupid fellow; a dotard; dolt; a poor creature; a mere animal; beast; a person devoid of all judgment, sense, or discretion. uṭhaṅ'gal mulk . E. A land of anarchy and misrule; an ill-governed state or kingdom.
s.f. Dulness, heaviness, stupidity, &c. (see uṭhangal).
s.m. Prop, support; anything placed under a vessel to prevent its upsetting, or under a body to keep it from rolling; a scotch-stone, wedge, &c. (also written uṭhgan).
more: s. m. A prop or support.

def: v.n. To rest (upon), lean (against), depend or rely (upon) for support; to be propped or supported; to be stopped or stayed.

E. v. n. 1. To lean against; to be propped up. Keu charhai baithai rukhawa, birichhawā. Ahe keu to uṭhaṅglai okar jaryā re kī . Mag. Song ( Pārbatī kī jantāsārī ). Some mount and sit on branches. and Some lean against the roots, or sit or stand. 2. To depend or rely upon.
s.m. Support; expenditure, expenses; running account (as with a baniyā); debit.
n. m. Something set apart to be offered to some god or goddess. Mātā kā uṭhāonā uṭhā rakkho .
s.f. A certain ceremony performed among Hindūs in honour of a deceased person on the third day after death. (The corresponding ceremony among Mohammadans is termed پهول phūl, q.v.)
n. f. Mah. phūl . A Hindu ceremony performed on the second or third day after death.
adj. & s.m. Extravagant, prodigal, lavish; — extravagant fellow, prodigal, spendthrift.

adj. Extravagant; profuse; lavish. —n. m. A spend-thrift; prodigal; an extravagant fellow. Phr. With an unsparing hand; money burning in one's pocket (khāū luṭāū) . uṭhāū chūlhā, lit . a travelling kitchen stove. One who is not settled; one who has no fixed or certain livelihood; a rolling stone.
v.t. = اٿهانا uṭhānā
subs: (uṭhāʼonā), Something set apart to be offered to a god or goddess.

def: s.f. Rising up and sitting down, not sitting still; restlessness, fidgetiness, agitation; tossing and tumbling; perturbation, discomposure; a kind of exercise among athletes; standing and kneeling alternately at prayer; sitting and rising alternately holding both ears the while, — a mode of punishment in Indian schools
more: s. f. 1. Restlessness. 2. A kind of exercise.
subs: v.n. To be constantly getting up and sitting down from uneasiness of mind; to suffer from restlessness; to have the fidgets.

def: n. f. 1. The opposite of sitting still. Uṭh baiṭh na kar, nichlā baiṭh . 2. Frequently getting up or leaving one's seat or business. Ḍug ḍug bāje, bahut nīkī lāge, Nauā neg māṅge, uṭṭhā baiṭhī lāge . E. Prov. To listen to the rub-a-dub how pleasant 'tis! The barber (drummer) payment asks—how fidgety one grows! 3. Standing and kneeling alternately at prayer. Chūṅ jī. yāī uṭhak baiṭhak ne navāj kahe haiṅ ? Rus. 4. A kind of athletic exercise ( baiṭhkī ). 5. A punishment in schools—the offender being made to hold both his ears and rise and sit alternately. 6. Restlessness; fidget; agitation; tossing and tumbling; disquietude; perturbation; discomposure. uṭh baiṭh lagnā , v. n. To be getting up and sitting down every moment from uneasiness of mind. Buā, khair to hai, kyoṅ uṭh baiṭh lag rahī hai ? Wom.
s.m. = اٿهنگن uṭhangan, q.v. s.m. Prop, support; anything placed under a vessel to prevent its upsetting, or under a body to keep it from rolling; a scotch-stone, wedge, &c. (also written uṭhgan).
part. adj. (from uthalnā), Upset, &c. The commoner form is uthal-puthal, or utthal-putthal, upset, capsized, overturned; topsy-turvy, in utter confusion, in complete disorder
subs: To be upset or overturned; to be topsy-turvy; to be disordered (the stomach).

def: adj. Shallow, low, shelving; superficial; unable to keep a secret.

ety: caus. of uthalnā, and i.q. uthālnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To invert, turn upside down, overturn, capsize, upset; to turn topsy-turvy, to throw into disorder or confusion; to toss, tumble, disarrange.
more: v. a. S. उत् up, स्था to place, H. auṅdhānā to upset. 1. To turn upside down; turn topsy-turvy; turn inside out; upset; turn over; overset; tumble and toss; disarrange. Sāre kapṛe uthal ḍāle . 2. To confuse; disorder; shuffle; muddle. 3. To raise by means of a lever; raise or remove from its place. Billī ne chhīpā uthal-kar malāī khā lī . E. The cat overturned the cover and ate up the cream. uthal jānā, ud'hal —, v. n. To feel the sexual passion so strong as to be unable to control oneself.
(caus. of اتهلنا) v. a. To cause to turn over or overset.

ety: S. उद्+स्था; pass. उत्थीय(ते), used actively; Prk. उट्ठे(इ)
def: v.n. To rise, rise up, stand up, get up (from bed, or from a sick-bed, — hence), to awake; to recover from illness; to ascend, mount, soar; to swell (as a river, &c.); to be inflamed (as the eyes); to be developed, to attain to puberty, to feel sexual desire (a female), to be in heat; to work, ferment (as leaven); to rise for the purpose of leaving (a place, — hence), to take leave, to leave, go away, set forth, come forth or be abroad (as a beast in quest of prey); to start; to move (from a house), quit, leave; to depart (from a place, or from this world); to be done away with, be abolished, be discontinued; to be removed, be carried away; to disappear; to be closed (as a shop or business); to be rubbed out, effaced, obliterated; to be laid out, expended, to cost; to be finished, be over; to be prepared, be ready; to be exhausted, spent; — to be raised, taken up, raised on high, (hence) to be exhibited; to come out distinctly, to appear (as type, &c.); to be erected, built, constructed, made, formed; to be reared, brought up, trained, educated; to be hatched; to spring up, sprout, shoot, grow; to proceed (from), originate, issue, emanate, result; to break out (as a sore, an epidemic, a mutiny, &c.), to arise (as a storm, a disturbance, &c.), to come on, begin (as pain, disease, &c.); to be started, be established (as any institution); to arise (out of), to accrue (as profit); to be derived, obtained, got, gained, realized; to be recovered; to be reclaimed or brought under cultivation; to be taken as a loan, or on credit, or on hire, or on lease; to be gathered in (as a harvest); to be drawn up, be absorbed (as ink by blotting-paper)
more: v. n. To rise up, be raised, spring; to be abolished.
subs: v.n. To get up, sit up, rise up, start up, wake up; to be revived, to gain strength, to recover

v.n. (intens. of uṭhnā), To be removed, done away with, abolished; to come to an end, be expended, finished, terminated, settled, &c.; to cease; to remove, quit, go away (from, -se), depart (from this world); to vanish, disappear
To rise or stand up suddenly

v.n. To be constantly rising up and sitting down; to be restless, uneasy, fidgetty, &c.; to be constantly going out; to be in the habit of visiting; to associate (with); to do anything leisurely or at one's ease.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀉𑀝𑁆𑀞𑀇 (uṭṭhaï), from Sanskrit *उत्थाति (*utthāti), or a back-formation from 𑀉𑀝𑁆𑀞𑀸𑀯𑁂𑀇 (uṭṭhāvei).

def: v. n. (dakh.) See اٿهنا uṭhnā.

v. a. 1. To work or do any thing leisurely. Ham to uṭhte baiṭhte chār āne kamā lete haiṅ . 2. To pay visits; to visit; to go into company. Chhoṛ de, Goryā, taiṅ uṭhnā baiṭhnā, chhoṛ de Maghī pān . E. Song. Brihā . Give up gadding, young beauty, give up Maggah pān . i. e. Don't redden your lips with betel leaf and go flirting about. 3. To associate; keep company with. 4. Used as a double entendre .
more: n. Ped. nishast barkhāst . Manners; etiquette; breeding. uṭh uṭh baiṭhnā , v. n. 1. To be continually rising from one's seat. 2. To be restless, uneasy, fidgety. 3. To be made to jump, or dance (from a whipping).

def: (Walī), v. n. In Comp . sudden action is implied, as bol uṭhā exclaimed, ro uṭhā cried out, uṭh chalā set out. 1. To originate; take its rise; begin; spring. Gadar kā jhagṛā Meraṭ se uṭṭhā . 2. To start; proceed; take one's way (chal paṛnā) . Lāṭ sāhab daure ko uṭṭhe . 3. To sprout; shoot; germinate. Paudā uṭhte hī jal gayā . 4. To grow; flourish; promise, or come on well. Bāṛī to bhalī uṭṭhī saë . Rus. 5. Result; issue; flow; come or grow out of; proceed from; arise out of; emanate; accrue; follow; come of. Khūb mazā uṭhā . 6. To come out of the egg; to be hatched. Murgī ke kaë bachche uṭhe? E. 7. To break forth or out. Fasād uṭṭhā . 8. To come out; break out (a sore). Phoṛā uṭṭhā . 9. To arise; to begin to blow; come on. Aṅdhī uṭṭhī, ghar bhāgo . 10. To begin; to come on. Khāṅsī uṭṭhī . 11. To rise; get up; stand up (khaṛā honā). Uṭhte lāt, baiṭhte ghūṅsā . Prov. Standing or sitting, a kick or a blow. 12. To rise; go; take leave; make a move; leave one's seat. Ab yahāṅ se āp uṭhye . 13. To wake; rise from bed ( jāgnā ). Uṅṭ ke beṭe ho (you are the son of camel), a pun on the phrase— Uṭh-ke baiṭhe ho (Get up). 14. To stand; to be erect. Tumhārī qismat se uṭṭhā hai, baiṭh jāo. (Double entendṛe) . 15. To be lifted, raised (one's feet). Tumhārā pāoṅ nahīṅ uṭhtā, kyā meṅhdī lagī hai? Step out, can't you? you have n't applied henna to your feet. 16. To be raised, built, constructed, piled up. 17. To raise; swell (a river). Gaṅgā bahut uṭhī hai . E. 18. To be in heat; to desire copulation. Āyā kātik, uṭṭhī kutyā . Prov. Come October, dogs in heat. 19. To grow to womanhood, or puberty; to grow large. Ab to chhokrī uṭhtī chalī . 20. To ferment; to be leavened. Khamīr uṭhyāyā . 21. To be carried, taken away, removed. Māl uṭṭhe, to jagah khālī ho . 22. To emerge; appear; come into view. 23. To ascend; mount; soar; fly (uṛnā). Lo, unke kabūtar uṭṭhe, tum bhī uṛāo . 24. To be expended or laid out; to cost. Shādī meṅ baṛā rupayā uṭṭhā . 25. To be taken off, discountinued, abolished. Ek ṭikas uṭhā, dūsrā lagā . 26. To spread; to become epidemic. Haizā uṭhā . 27. To issue (in quest of prey). 1. Sher uṭṭhā hai, chaukas rahnā . E. 2. Bairī uṭṭhī, kabūtār mat uṛāo . 28. To be impressed, printed, engraved. Ṭhappā achchhā nahīṅ uṭṭhā . 29. To be read, deciphered, made out. Waë bhaī! Urdū ke harf bhī nahīṅ uṭhte . 30. To be reaped, cut, gathered (harvest). 1. Khet uṭhne pe nāj sastā hogā . 2. Pair uṭṭhe pe deṅge, Lālā . Rus. 31. To be rubbed out, effaced, obliterated. Is rabar se harf nahīṅ uṭhtā . 32. To be sucked up, absorbed. Is syāī-chaṭ * se aṅgrezī nahīṅ uṭhtī . 33. To let; lease; to be farmed (land). Kis paṛte pe dhartī uṭṭhī hai? 34. To gain; obtain; get; acquire (hāth lagnā). Kyā nafā uṭṭhā? 35. To be brought up, educated, trained. Unke bachche sab achchhe uṭṭhe . Wom. 36. To be cooked or prepared; to acquire flavour, taste, etc. ( savād par ānā ). Achār achchhā uṭṭhā hai . * Blotting paper. 37. To recover; to get well. Khudā hī hai jo mujh sā bīmār uṭṭhe . 38. To be ready, prepared, etc; to be on the alert. 1. Koi uṭṭhe yā na uṭṭhe, ham to tayār haiṅ . 2. Sab rājā muqāble ko uṭṭhe . 39. To be taken out or exhibited (as in a procession, etc.). 1. Ṭesū uṭṭhā . 2. Tāzyā baṛī dhūm se uṭṭhā . 40. To stir, or exert oneself. Uṭh-kar phalī nahīṅ phoṛte . Prov. He won't even raise his hand to his mouth. (said of extreme indolence). 41. To take to one's heels; to take flight; to show the white feather. Fauj ke pair uṭh gaë . 42. To be instituted, established, opened. Ek maktab aur uṭṭhā . 43. To be lent, advanced, laid out at interest. Sāh jī, kāhe ghabṛāël hā, jittā rupayā uṭhā hai, dām dām bhar deīṅ . E. Rus. 44. To finish; complete; turn out. Taṛke se mār mār kī, jab sāṅjh ko ek kurtā uṭṭhā . Hin. Wom. 45. To bear; support; endure. Mujh se itnā bojh nahīṅ uṭhegā . 46. To be over; to be adjourned or dissolved; to rise. 1. Peṅth uṭhne pe tum āë . 2. Kacherī uṭṭhī . 47. To die; cease to live. Āj Kūṛā jahāṅ se uṭṭhā . Zauq. 48. To leave; quit; vacate (a lodging). Tum uṭṭho, to makān khālī ho . 49. To get a price for; to realize. 1 Mukdam , 2 jhoṭī kā kahā uṭho ? Rus. 50. To come on (as pain); shoot; throb. Uṭhī, morī guiyāṅ, kalejwā meṅ pīr . Song. Dādrā .
s.m. Deportment, manners, breeding; etiquette ( = P. nishast-barḵẖāst).
adj. (f. -ī), Rising, ascending; opening budding; developing; flourishing; — declining, waning fading, decaying
subs: adv. Lit. 'rising and sitting'; Easily, gradually, little by little; leisurely; slowly; — quickly, in a trice; incessantly, perpetually.

def: adv. lit . standing and sitting. 1. Gradually; little by little; bit by bit; inch by inch; by degrees; now and again. Uṭhte baiṭhte tumhārā bhī kām kar dūṅgā . 2. Slowly; hardly; at a slow pace; with great difficulty; walking and resting alternately; by fits and starts; leisurely. Uṭhte baiṭhte wahāṅ pahuṅch gaë . 3. Quickly; easily; in a short space of time. Kyā baṛā kām hai, uṭhte baiṭhte kar dūṅgā . 4. Perpetually; incessantly; every moment. Uṭhte baiṭhte kostī hai . She curses night and day.

adj. 1. Rising; opening; flourishing. 1 The head of a village. 2 A young buffalo. 2. Declining; dying; languishing; decaying; waning; fading. Bīmārī meṅ na āë, uṭhtī lāsh pe āë. uṭhtī peṅṭh . Applied to declining age, fortune, etc. Uṭhī peṅṭh āṭhweṅ din . Prov. Market day comes once a week. (Make hay while the sun shines). uṭhtī jawānī,—huī —, n. f. Breasts almost fully formed or developed; the prime of youth. Uṭthī huī jawānī kā ubhrā huā sīnā, Kyā gāt hai, kyā gāt hai, kyā gāt hai, Wallāh! Jurat. Fast growing puberty, high-swelling breasts, What a bust, what a bust, what a bust, by Heaven! uṭhtī shah'wat , n. f. Rising lust, or passion. Us josh se uṭhtī shahwat meṅ phir ā-kar dhīṅgā-mushtī ho. Woh hallā kar-ke ān paṛe, aur yāṅ shahwat kī pushtī ho. Kuchh hāt pakaṛ, kuch chhātī mal, kuch mụṅh meṅ hushtā hushtī ho. Ban jāë akhāṛā ashrat kā, aur aish tarab kī kushtī ho. Sab tan ke hāṛ khaṛakte hoṅ, jab dekh bahāreṅ Holī kī . (A description of the great national festival, called Holī ). Nazīr. And as the excitement stronger grew, Each one the other, pulls about, There with a rush they fall on these, Here passion roused is their defence, Now arms are seized, now breasts are squeezed, "For shame" comes faintly from some lips. Pleasure's palæstra here you see, Her sons and maids in amorous strife, Each bone in every body cracks, Now is the height of Holī's joys. uṭhtī ko'pal , n. f. 1. New, or growing shoot or germ. 2. Budding youth; puberty; rising breasts. 1. Uṭhtī kopal hai jawānī kī, nayā hai andāz, Hoṅṭh patle haiṅ, maseṅ bhīgtī, sabzā āgāz . Jān Sāhab. The budding bloom of youth is his, and grace unique, Thin lips, moustache like down, and whiskers young and sleek. 2. Būṭā sā hai qad uskā, aur chāl meṅ chhalbal hai, Ho kyoṅ na bahār us par, uṭhtī huī kopal hai . Jurat. Small [as a fairy] she, and sportive, frisky in her gait, The bloom of spring must needs infold a bud so delicate.

ety: from uṭhwā-nā; — iyā = S. aff. इक
def: s.m. One who rises, one who goes away; one who raises or helps to raise (a load, &c.); one who spends money lavishly, an extravagant person, a prodigal ( = uṭhāʼū).
n. 1. One who helps to lift or raise a load. 2. A prodigal, extravagant person. E. Tū baṛā uṭhwaiyā āyā, mere paise uṭhā ḍāle . E.
v. a. To cause to be lifted or raised up, removed, etc.
v.t. To cause to be lifted or raised or removed, &c.
more: (caus. of اٿهنا) v. a. To cause to lift or raise up, or remove, &c.

def: adj. Thrown off, cast off, worn (as clothes, &c.).
s.m. Thought, care; a porter, one who travels with a burden or load; a fowler; a name of the province of Orissa (Urīsā).
more: n. prop. A name of the province of Urīsā.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Having the neck uplifted; eagerly longing for; regretting, missing.
s.f. Longing for anything; regretting; uneasiness, care, sorrow.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Regretting; missing; wishing; sorrowing for; regretful, sorrowful, uneasy; — one who longs after an absent love.
s.m. = S اتکرشتا उत्कर्षता utkarshatā, s.f. Elevation, exaltation, eminence, excellence, superiority; distinction; excess.
s.f. = S اتکرش उत्कर्ष utkarsha, s.m. Elevation, exaltation, eminence, excellence, superiority; distinction; excess.
adj. (f. -ā) & s.m. Exceeding the usual measure, excessive; abounding in; drunk; mad; furious; proud; difficult; — intoxication; pride; an elephant in rut; Laurus cassia or its fragrant bark.
adj. (f. -ā), Superior; exalted; excellent, good.
= اتاولا utāwalā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Swift, quick, speedy; precipitate, hasty, rash; uneasy, fidgety, restless, impatient
adj. (f. -ī), As much as that, that much, so much, so many
more: adj. As much as that; so much; so many; to that extent or amount; that much. utnā hī, itnā hī , adv. Neither more nor less; exactly.
adj. m. As much as that, so much, so many.
subs: adv. (emphat. of utnā), Just as much as that, exactly so much or many
adv. As many as that, so many.

def: equally
oblique plural of اُون٘ٹ (ū̃ṭ, “camel”)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Causing, originating, producing; — originator, father; a certain fabulous animal with eight legs.
s.m. Producing, creating.
s.m. A species of blue lotus, Nymphæa cerulea; a certain plant (Costus).
part. Sprung, originated, produced, born, risen, resulted, gained; — s.m. Origin, creation, production, birth, rise, appearance; produce; proceeds; profit, gain
more: n. m. 1. Birth; ereation; origin. 2. Extraction; descent; family. 3. Appearance; phenomenon; portent. 4. Produce; proceeds ( paidā ). 5. The realized rents or profits (of a village); produce of the harvest. ut'pann honā, ut'pat honā , v. a. 1. To be born or produced; to come into the world. 2. To yield; produce.
adj. Produced; s. Proceeds, profit, produce.
subs: To be produced, come into being, be born, &c.; to arise, result, occur.

def: s.m. Portent, phenomenon; distress; confusion; violence, injustice; injury, mischief. — utpāt karnā, To do wrong, violence, &c.; to contend; to be insolent; to answer, retort.
more: n. m. Violence; injury; injustice ( upaddar ). ut'put k . v. a. To contend; answer; retort.
s. m. A portent, phenomenon; violence, injustice, injury.

def: s.m. Plucking out, eradicating, &c. (see upāṛnā).
adj. & s.m. = ūṭ-paṭāng, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = ūṭ-paṭāng, q.v.
s. m. Absurdity, nonsense, what is without meaning or foundation. adj. Poor, helpless, destitute, in disorder.

adj. Poor, helpless, miserable; useless, worthless; confused, in disorder; senseless, nonsensical, absurd, ridiculous; crabbed, hard to deal with, difficult to manage; — s.m. Anything without meaning or without foundation, nonsense, absurdity.
s.m. One who speaks inconsiderately or without preparation; a wretched creature; a senseless fellow.
s.m. = ūt-paṭāngī, q.v. s.m. One who speaks inconsiderately or without preparation; a wretched creature; a senseless fellow.
adj. (used substantively) One who speaks inconsiderately or without preparation.
adj. (f. -inī), Violent, destructive, &c. (see utpāt).
s.f. Origin, production, birth, springing up; rise; title of the book of Genesis
more: s. f. Birth, origin, creation, production.
subs: s.m. An epithet of the Supreme as the originator of creation

def: table
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit उत्पीठिका (utpīṭhikā).

def: s.f. Indifference, carelessness; (in Rhet. & Poetry) comparison; illustration.

part. adj. Worn, aged, faded; dismissed from office; degraded, disgraced, &c. — utrā shahna mardak nām, prov. A dismissed superintendent of police is styled (or considered) a nobody, 'out of place out of grace.'
more: adj. Reduced; dismissed; out of place. Utrā shahnā, mardak nām . Prov. Out of place, out of grace.

def: s.f. North wind (cf. uttarāhā).

s.f. Coming down, alighting; requital, return; price paid for carrying or conveying over (a river, bridge, &c.), ferriage, toll; decline of life; discharge (of a debt, &c.); draft, cheque, order (for payment of money)
more: adj. 1. Affected; playful; wanton; coquettish. 2. Swaggering; showing off; presuming. 3. Arrogant; high and mighty; consequential; on stilts; purse proud.

n. f. Coming down; alighting. 1. Requital; grateful return; retribution. 2. The toll paid at a ferry. utrāī ke din , Opp. of chaṛhāī ke din . The close of the season; the exodus from the hills. utar paṛ'nā , v. n. 1. To alight upon; descend; come down; fall upon ( utarnā ). 2. To lodge; put up at; halt (on a journey); stop on the way. utartā belā,—pahil,— —kī, (Ṭhagī.), ḍhalte paher , Declining day; afternoon. utar'tā chāṅd , n. m. The moon's wane ( badī ).

def: v. n. (dakh.) To be grateful, to return thanks or to have discharged duty or gratitude.
s.f. A medicinal lactescent plant, Cynanchum extensum. (Also written utrang in Western and Southern India.)
more: n. f. A medicinal lactescent plant, Cynanchum extensum . Wat.
n. f. Worn out or cast off clothes.
see under اترنا utarnā ) s. f. A fragment, any thing taken off from the body; cast off clothes. اترن هونا utaran-honā, To be freed (from debt), to descend, alight.

def: E. v. n. To be freed (from debt or an obligation).
s.f. A coming out (of), being freed (from); anything taken off from the body, worn-out or cast-off clothes; a fragment. — utran honā (-se), To be freed (from), quit (of), out (of debt). (See next.)

v.t. To cause to be taken off or down; to cause to be pulled down (in these senses اتروانا utarwānā is the common form); — to boil up or over; to froth, foam; to rest on the surface (of a liquid), to float: — s.m. A floating body; a float.
more: (caus. of اترنا q. v.) v. a. 1. To take down or pull down (by the hands of a third person; as, to do it oneʼs self, is اتارنا utārnā ). 2. To boil over.

def: s.m. A wooden arch surmounting a door; the lintel of a door.
adj. (f. -ā), Freed from obligation, out of debt.
s.m. = اتسو utsav, q.v. s.m. Public demonstration of joy, rejoicing, festival; jubilee; — impatience, wrath.
more: n. m. S. उत्सव, S. उत् + सु to perform a sacrifice, Pr. ussavo a feast. A public demonstration of joy; jubilee; festival.

def: s.m. Joy, gladness, pleasure; labour, effort, perseverance, energy, fortitude.
more: s. m. Effort, perseverance, energy, fortitude; happiness, joy.

def: adj. (f. -inī), Joyous, rejoicing; energetic, persevering.
s.m. The lap; the slope of a mountain; a roof.
s.m. Abandoning, resigning; giving; consecrating; a rule or precept.
s.m. Public demonstration of joy, rejoicing, festival; jubilee; — impatience, wrath.
more: s. m. A festival, a jubilee, publick rejoicing.

def: adj. (f. -ī) and adv. That much or many, &c. (see اتنا utnā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَتَا (atā), from a Turkic language.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Uppermost, highest, supreme, chief, principal, first, most excellent, best, greatest; last; great; high
more: adj. Superl. of उत. 1. Good; excellent; best; first rate; tip top. Uttam khetī, maddham bān, Nikhad chākrī, bhīk nidān . Prov. Best is tillage; middling, trade; Base is service; begging, worst. 2. Virtuous; good; respectable; superior. Uttam se uttam mile, mile nīch se nīch; Pānī se pānī mile, mile kīch se kīch. Dohā . Good joins with good, and bad with bad; With water, water mixeth, mire with mire. 3. Chief; matchless; peerless; unrivalled; none such. Phr. A gem of the first water. Sonā kahe sunār se, "uttam mhārī jāt, Kāle mūṅh kī ghuṅgchī, aur tule hamāre sāth"! Dohā . "My rank is highest," to the goldsmith, quoth the gold, "Seat in the scale with me that black faced, ghunghchī * bold?" uttam purush , n. m. 1. A good virtuous man. 2. In Gram . The first person; the person speaking.
adj. Excellent, best, principal, first, chief.
subs: s.m. An excellent man, &c.; (in gram.) the first person.

def: s.f. Excellence; super-excellence; superiority; greatness; worth.

r: uttān, utān, ā, ćit, aundhā, Lit.
def: = H اتان उतान utān, Adj. (f. -ā), Lying on the back; lying supinely on the broad of the back (syn. ćit); inverted, upside down (a vessel, &c.
subs: rugged; conceited, proud (Lit. "having the face upwards").

def: adj. (f. -ā), Anxious, excited.
s.m. Placing one thing over another.
more: s. m. (dakh.) Placing one upon another. اتڙکي اتڙ uttaṛ ke uttaṛ, One upon another.

def: Uttarakhand (a state of India)
adj. North-west.
n. f. North-west wind; tradewinds. ut'tar de' nā , v. n. To answer; to make, or give answer; reply.
Uttar Pradesh (a state in northern India)
wety: From اُتَّر (uttar, “north”) + پَرَدیش (paradeś, “province”).

def: adj. (f. of uttar), Northern, northerly; to the north; — s.f. North wind, Boreas.
more: adj. Northern; northerly. —n. f. The north wind; Boreas.
adj. Northern, northerly; s. f. North wind, boreas.

def: adj. (f. -ā), & s.m. Farther; posterior; last; lofty; upper; surpassing, superior; more powerful; excellent; best; left (not right); answering; succeeding, subsequent; future; northerly
subs: defence; superiority; power; arrival; the north; consequence; (in arith.) difference. — uttarādhikār (i.e. ˚ra+adhi˚), s.m. Inheritance, succession

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Inheriting, succeeding; — heir, successor. — uttarārdh (˚ra+ardha), s.m. Latter half (of a book, &c.), last part; upper part (of the body); northern part; further end. — uttarāshāṛha, s.f. The twenty-first Nakshatra or lunar mansion, figured by an elephant's tooth or a bed, and containing two stars, one of which is β Sagittarius. — uttarāyana (˚ra+ay˚), s.m. The sun's progress to the north of the equator, northing; the summer-solstice. — uttara-bhādra-pad, s.m. The twenty-sixth lunar mansion, figured by a couch, and comprehending two stars, one of which is Andromedæ. — uttar-paśćim (or — paććhim, or paććham), adj. & s.m. north-west; the north-west

s.f. north-west wind; trade-wind. — uttara-phalgunī (or phālgunī), s.f. The twelfth lunar mansion, figured by a bed, and containing two stars. — uttar-dāyī (or-dāʼī), adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), answering; — one who gives a reply. — uttar-diśā, s.f. Northerly direction; the north. — uttar-kārī, adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), making answer; — one who replies. — uttar-kāṇḍ, s.m. The title of the seventh and last section of the Rāmāyan. — uttara-khaṇḍ, s.m. The concluding book of the Padma-Purāṇa, and of the Śiva-Purāṇa; — adj. Of Northern India. — uttara-lakshaṇa, s.m. The characteristics of a reply or defence (which are, 1˚ That it be applicable to the whole charge: 2˚ That it be maintainable by law: 3˚ That it be not equivocal or evasive: 4˚ That it be consistent with itself: 5˚ That it be perspicuously expressed). — uttarottar (˚ra+ut˚), adv. More and more; higher and higher; further and further; — s.m. Reply to an answer, rejoinder. — uttara-vartī, adj. (f. -inī), Existing in the north; northern.

def: n. m. The twenty-sixth lunar mansion, figured by a couch and comprehending two stars, of which one is Andromedœ .
s. m. A mansion of the moon (the twenty-seventh).
For compounds of which uttara or uttar is the first member, v.s.v. اتر uttara.
adj. (f. -ī), Northern; appertaining to the North.

n. m. Ped. The characteristics of a reply, viz., that it be applicable to the whole charge; that it be maintainable by law; that it be not equivocal or evasive; that it be consistent with itself; and that it be perspicuously expressed.
n. f. The twelfth lunar mansion, containing two stars figured by a bed.
s.m. A place to which fish throng.
more: s. m. A place to which fish throng.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Northern; appertaining to the North.
n. m. Ped. Northing; the sun's northern declination, or passage from the southern to the northern limit of the ecliptic; the summer solstice.
more: s. m. Northing, passing (of the sun) from the south to the north.

def: northern; farther; upper
s.m. An upper or outer garment.

s.f. = اٿهہ بيٿهہ uṭh-baiṭh, q.v. s.f. Rising up and sitting down, not sitting still; restlessness, fidgetiness, agitation; tossing and tumbling; perturbation, discomposure; a kind of exercise among athletes; standing and kneeling alternately at prayer; sitting and rising alternately holding both ears the while, — a mode of punishment in Indian schools
subs: v.n. To be constantly getting up and sitting down from uneasiness of mind; to suffer from restlessness; to have the fidgets.

def: adj. Shallow, low, shelving; superficial; unable to keep a secret.
adj. S. ष्ठा, H. thā stand. 1. Shallow; low ( chhichhorā ). 2. Not able to keep a secret; gossiping; talebearing ( peṭ kā halkā ).

s.m. A rising up, awaking from sleep; effort, manly exertion; battle; joy. — utthān ekādashī, or devotthān, The 11th of the śukl-paksh of Kārtik, when Vishnu awakes from sleep. During the months that he sleeps it is unlawful to marry.
more: n. f. 1. Rising; rise; ascent; ascension; pitch. 2. Height; elevation; altitude; eminence; top; summit; lofty flight. Yeh kabūtar baṛī uṭhān ke haiṅ . 3. Growth; tallness; size. Is laṛkī kī gazab uṭhān hai, koī dinoṅ baṛhtā hai, yeh ghaṛyoṅ baṛhtī hai . 4. Make; figure; build; body ( angleṭ ). Iskī uṭhān hī aisī hai . 5. Puberty; swelling of the breasts (a girl's). 6. Sexual desire (of the female). 7. Swagger; foppishness; upstartishness. 8. Expenses; expenditure. E. nayā uṭhān , n. m. Promotion; rise; advancement; sudden elevation. Nayā uṭhān in kar bhail bā . Bhoj. (Said of one whose head is turned by his sudden elevation).

s. Effort, rising up, manly exertion, battle, joy. उत्थान एकादशी utthān ekādasī, or देवठान devaṭhān, The eleventh of the sukl paksh̤ of Kārtik, when Vishnu awakes from sleep. (During the four months that he sleeps it is unlawful to marry).
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit उत्थान (utthāna). May also have been inherited through Prakrit [Term?] 𑀉𑀢𑁆𑀣𑀸𑀡 (utthāṇa).

ety: S. उद+स्थापन, caus. of rt. स्था
def: s.m. The act of raising a thing up, erecting, raising; tossing up; (in Arith.) producing an answer or result.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Landed, crossed, arrived; escaped, rescued, liberated; thrown off.
s.m. Raising, lifting.
n. m. Northern country.
s.m. Plaiting, puckering, crimping, gathering into ornamental plaits or folds; marking; — plaits or folds of cloth
more: n. m. A. اُطو folding. 1. Plaiting; puckering; gathering into ornamental plaits or folds; marking. 2. Plaits or folds of cloth. ut'tū kar'nā , v. a. 1. To plait; pucker; crease; crimp. Aṅgarkhe pe uttū kar do . 2. To drag one's foot along the ground like a lame man; to trail. Chaltā kyā hai, zamīn pe uttū kartā hai! Phabtī . (Chaff.). Walking you call it? Say he makes creases on the ground (with his lame foot). 3. To beat severely so as to leave marks on the body; to beat one to a mummy. Mār-kar uttū kar dȳā, (yā banā) dȳā . 4. To make one drunk; to make a fool of one ( ullū banānā ). 1. Do hī bātoṅ meṅ uttū kar dȳā . In two words I made him look foolish. 2. Bhaṅg ne uttū kar dȳā . 5. To abuse; vilify; inveigh, or rail against. 6. To dishonor; disgrace; bring into disrepute. ut'tū-gar,—kash , n. One who plaits cloth. ut'tū ho'nā , v. n. 1 To become intoxicated; not to know whether on…

s. m. Plaits of cloth. مارکر اتو بنایا اسکیتئین mārkar uttū banāyā uske taʼīṅ, He beat him to a mummy (lit. till his body became marked like the plaits in cloth). اتو کرنا uttū karnā, v. a. To plait. اتو کش uttū-kash, or اتو گر uttū-gar, A plaiter.
subs: v.t. To streak, mark, or wale (the body by beating), to turn (one) into a mass of wales (by severe beating; — e.g. mārkar uttū banāya uske-taʼīṅ, 'he beat him to a mummy')

v.t. To plait, pucker, crimp, &c.; to streak or wale (the body by beating = uttū banānā); to make a fool of; to stupefy, besot; to overwhelm with raillery or abuse; to vilify, disgrace, dishonour, bring into disrepute
and uttū-gar, s.m. One who plaits, &c., one who makes ornamental folds in cloth
To become silly, or stupid, or stupefied, or besotted, &c.; to be disgraced, to lose repute or character, to fall into disrepute.

def: = P اتوکش uttū-kash, v.s.v. اتو uttū.
= P اتوگر uttū-gar, v.s.v. اتو uttū.
